posts.label: Jun 23, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - Know one part of the tech stack in great detail. Be the go-to person. Patterns repeat up and down the stack, so once you truly understand something deeply, you start recognizing the same shapes everywhere else, and every new thing you learn comes faster. Depth in one place becomes leverage in all the others. 🙏

posts.label: Jun 10, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - Share information with context. Do not hoard knowledge or build silos to feel important. The person who connects the dots, explains the why, and brings others along creates leverage for the entire team. Context turns information into impact.

posts.label: Jun 2, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - Good news can wait, bring the bad news first. We’ll solve issues together and have more time to celebrate later.

posts.label: May 26, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - Be the calmest person in the room. When incidents happen, tempers flare, or chaos and panic start spreading, slow down and stay focused. Calm creates clarity and clarity improves decision making when the pressure is on. Teams trust the people who can steady the room when it matters most.

posts.label: May 19, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - Ask, what can I do to help you? The people who create lift for others create momentum for the whole team.

posts.label: May 12, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - If you see something, say something. A metric drifting, a product gap, a UX inconsistency, a system that needs fixing, people out of alignment, behavior that needs correction. Call it out clearly and constructively. The people who protect the standard and move the whole team forward are the ones who create lasting impact.

posts.label: May 4, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - It's not that you made a mistake, it's what you do next that counts. Everyone makes mistakes, that's not what defines you. Own it fast, fix it fully, and share what you learned so the team gets stronger. Those who recover well earn more trust than those who never stumble.

posts.label: Apr 28, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - Close the loop. When something ships, lands, or changes, follow through and share the outcome. What worked, what did not, what we learned. People who help the team learn in the open drive more impact.

posts.label: Apr 23, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - raise the standard quietly. Fix the doc, improve the system, make the code consistent, clean up loose ends, all without being asked. Small quality moves compound, and people notice who consistently leaves things better than they found them.

posts.label: Apr 20, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - Constraints breed creativity. Limits force focus, clarity, and better decisions. When resources are tight, priorities get sharper and solutions get smarter. Don’t fight constraints, use them to prioritize and make even more impact than expected.

posts.label: Apr 9, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - Follow up. If you say you'll do something, do it, promptly, ideally within minutes. If the task takes longer, tell people when they should expect the follow up, and then hit that timeline. Build a reputation as someone who delivers, and it compounds quickly. 🙌

posts.label: Apr 6, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - Run toward the fire. When there is an incident or something breaks, do not wait for direction or assume someone else has it covered. Lean in, help triage, communicate calmly and clearly, and stay with the problem until it is resolved. The people who show up first and stay through the fix become the ones others trust when it matters most. 🙏

posts.label: Apr 2, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - Be the one who fills the coffee maker, puts their dishes away, cleans up the conference room. Show common courtesies at work and watch it pay dividends. As Stewart Butterfield says, "Tilt your umbrella." 🙌

posts.label: Mar 24, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - Every day, one step better. Better at building technology, better at leading with intention, better at being the teammate who raises the bar for everyone. That kind of impact compounds exponentially and gets noticed. 🙏

posts.label: Mar 20, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - With change comes opportunity. When a reorg or strategy shift hits, do not just protect your lane. Rethink the strategy, find where you can help, and step into the gap while things are still fluid. Leaders value people who align execution and strategy. 📈

posts.label: Mar 17, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - Choose where you work based on two things: passion for the product and quality of leadership. Everything else is downstream. Great leaders can find product:market fit. Poor leaders can turn gold into rust. Fish rots from the head down. 🙏

posts.label: Mar 13, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - Be a reporter, not the story. Unless the news is yours, let the owner of it own the narrative. Don't spin it. Don't reshape it. Do this consistently and people learn that when news comes from you, it's accurate, important, and unfiltered. 🙌

posts.label: Mar 10, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - be in the details. Go beyond your comfort zone and learn more about the work behind the work, the edge cases, the tradeoffs, the "why." Detailed work is visible work, and visible work builds trust and impact. 🌟

posts.label: Mar 6, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - be dependable. Do what you said you would do on-time, every single time. Or give plenty of notice that you won't make the deliverable and commit to when you will. People notice. 🌟

posts.label: Mar 2, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - know your product deeply. Understand how what you are building connects to the rest of the system, avoid building in silos, and know how changes in one area affect others. Make sure the pieces work well together, not just in isolation. Use your product every single day to stay grounded in reality. 🙏

posts.label: Feb 26, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - Be the person who simplifies the complex. Do not use big words to try to sound important. There is real power in being able to explain things clearly, simply, and in terms people understand. 🙏

posts.label: Feb 23, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - Be coachable. The people who adapt to the rhythms and behaviors of their team consistently do more impactful work, because alignment creates momentum, and momentum compounds impact. 📈 🙌

posts.label: Feb 19, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - be on-time to meetings. Be the dependable one and respect people's time. If you're going to be even 2 mins late, send a quick DM.

posts.label: Feb 18, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - Own your mistakes quickly. The mistake isn't the problem. Hiding it is. What you do next is what counts.

posts.label: Feb 3, 2026
posts.text: Career hack - when a valued person leaves an org, treat them with the same respect and kindness on the way out as on the way in. I’ve been ghosted right away and I have been treated well. Guess which one I still praise and advocate for. The world is small, connections run deep.
